annotations.label: Geodesic (locally shortest path)

anchor.snippet: is a curve representing in some sense the locally

annotations.note: Mathlib has no definition of a geodesic; only curve-length/distance infrastructure (pathELength, riemannianEDist) exists.

annotations.label: Vanishing geodesic curvature characterization

anchor.snippet: geodesics are characterised by the property of having vanishing geodesic curvature

annotations.note: Geodesic curvature is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Geodesic via length minimization

anchor.snippet: can be defined by using the equation for the length of a curve

annotations.note: The length-minimizing distance riemannianEDist is formalized, but geodesics as length-minimizing curves are not defined.

annotations.label: Non-uniqueness of distance minimizers

anchor.snippet: It is possible that several different curves between two points minimize the distance

annotations.note: No notion of distance-minimizing geodesic is formalized, so non-uniqueness is not stated.

annotations.label: Segment of a geodesic is a geodesic

anchor.snippet: A contiguous segment of a geodesic is again a geodesic

annotations.note: Geodesics are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Geodesics are only locally shortest

anchor.snippet: geodesics are only locally the shortest distance between points

annotations.note: Geodesics and their local-minimization property are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Long way round on a great circle

anchor.snippet: Going the "long way round" on a great circle between two points on a sphere is a geodesic but not the shortest path

annotations.note: Neither great circles as geodesics nor this example exist in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Reparameterized unit-interval map is not a geodesic

anchor.snippet: from the unit interval on the real number line to itself gives the shortest path between 0 and 1

annotations.note: No geodesic notion exists, so this reparameterization example is not formalized.

annotations.label: Straight lines in Euclidean geometry

anchor.snippet: The most familiar examples are the straight lines in Euclidean geometry

annotations.note: Mathlib has line segments but no statement that straight lines are geodesics.

annotations.label: Great circles on a sphere

anchor.snippet: the images of geodesics are the great circles

annotations.note: Great circles as images of geodesics are not formalized.

annotations.label: Antipodal points: infinitely many shortest paths

anchor.snippet: then there are infinitely many shortest paths between them

annotations.note: No geodesics on spheres are formalized.

annotations.label: Geodesics on an ellipsoid

anchor.snippet: Geodesics on an ellipsoid behave in a more complicated way than on a sphere

annotations.note: Geodesics on ellipsoids are not formalized.

annotations.label: Geodesic triangle

anchor.snippet: A geodesic triangle is formed by the geodesics joining each pair out of three points on a given surface

annotations.note: Geodesic triangles are not defined (Mathlib only has affine/Euclidean triangles).

annotations.label: Spherical triangle

anchor.snippet: the geodesics are great circle arcs, forming a spherical triangle

annotations.note: Spherical triangles of great-circle arcs are not formalized.

annotations.label: Geodesic in metric geometry

anchor.snippet: a geodesic is a curve which is everywhere locally a distance minimizer

annotations.note: Mathlib has no geodesic-metric-space definition (no IsGeodesic / GeodesicSpace).

annotations.label: Minimizing geodesic / shortest path

anchor.snippet: the geodesic is called a minimizing geodesic or shortest path

annotations.note: No notion of minimizing geodesic / shortest path between points is defined.

annotations.label: Metric space may have no geodesics

anchor.snippet: a metric space may have no geodesics, except constant curves

annotations.note: Geodesic metric spaces are not formalized, so this remark is absent.

annotations.label: Length space: minimizing sequence of paths

anchor.snippet: any two points in a length metric space are joined by a minimizing sequence of rectifiable paths

annotations.note: Length metric spaces are not formalized as a structure in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Metric Hopf–Rinow theorem

anchor.snippet: The metric Hopf-Rinow theorem provides situations where a length space is automatically a geodesic space

annotations.note: The Hopf–Rinow theorem is not in Mathlib (no matches for Hopf-Rinow).

annotations.label: Geodesic metric spaces that are not manifolds

anchor.snippet: Common examples of geodesic metric spaces that are often not manifolds include metric graphs

annotations.note: Metric graphs as geodesic spaces are not formalized (Quiver.geodesicSubtree is unrelated tree combinatorics).

annotations.label: Length of a curve

anchor.snippet: of a continuously differentiable curve

annotations.note: pathELength defines a path's length as the integral of the norm of its derivative, generalizing the Riemannian length to enormed/Finsler tangent spaces.

annotations.label: Riemannian distance

anchor.snippet: is defined as the infimum of the length taken over all continuous, piecewise continuously differentiable curves

annotations.note: riemannianEDist is the infimum of pathELength over C¹ paths between two points, generalizing the Riemannian distance.

annotations.label: Geodesics are locally distance-minimizing (converse false)

anchor.snippet: all geodesics are locally distance-minimizing paths, but the converse is not true

annotations.note: Geodesics are undefined, so their local-minimization property is not formalized.

annotations.label: Characterization of geodesics among minimizers

anchor.snippet: only paths that are both locally distance minimizing and parameterized proportionately to arc-length are geodesics

annotations.note: No geodesic characterization exists; only arc-length reparameterization (naturalParameterization) is available separately.

annotations.label: Energy functional definition of geodesics

anchor.snippet: to define them as the minima of the following action or energy functional

annotations.note: The energy/action functional for curves is not formalized.

annotations.label: Cauchy–Schwarz length/energy inequality

anchor.snippet: the Cauchy–Schwarz inequality gives

annotations.note: The Cauchy–Schwarz inequality is formalized in general, but its integral application bounding length by energy is not.

annotations.label: Energy minimizers also minimize length

anchor.snippet: because they turn out to be affinely parameterized, and the inequality is an equality

annotations.note: The relation between energy and length minimizers is not formalized.

annotations.label: Geodesic equation (Euler–Lagrange)

anchor.snippet: This is the geodesic equation

annotations.note: The geodesic equation and Christoffel symbols are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: First variation of energy

anchor.snippet: The first variation of energy is defined in local coordinates by

annotations.note: The first variation of energy is not formalized.

annotations.label: Critical points of first variation are geodesics

anchor.snippet: The critical points of the first variation are precisely the geodesics

annotations.note: Neither the first variation nor geodesics are defined, so this characterization is absent.

annotations.label: Second variation

anchor.snippet: The second variation is defined by

annotations.note: The second variation of energy is not formalized.

annotations.label: Zeros of second variation are Jacobi fields

anchor.snippet: In an appropriate sense, zeros of the second variation along a geodesic

annotations.note: Jacobi fields are not formalized (no matches in Mathlib).

annotations.label: Geodesics as Hamiltonian flows

anchor.snippet: one can also regard geodesics as Hamiltonian flows

annotations.note: Hamiltonian flows of geodesics are not formalized.

annotations.label: Affine geodesic (parallel transport)

anchor.snippet: such that parallel transport along the curve preserves the tangent vector to the curve

annotations.note: Covariant derivatives (affine connections) are formalized, but parallel transport and the affine geodesic equation are not.

annotations.label: Unique solution from initial position and velocity

anchor.snippet: It has a unique solution, given an initial position and an initial velocity

annotations.note: ODE existence/uniqueness exists generally, but the geodesic equation it would apply to is not defined.

annotations.label: Local existence and uniqueness of geodesics

anchor.snippet: The local existence and uniqueness theorem for geodesics states that geodesics on a smooth manifold with an affine connection exist, and are unique

annotations.note: Local existence/uniqueness of geodesics is not formalized (geodesics are undefined).

annotations.label: Geodesic completeness

anchor.snippet: Any γ extends to all of ℝ if and only if M is geodesically complete

annotations.note: Geodesic completeness is not formalized.

annotations.label: Geodesic flow

anchor.snippet: Geodesic flow is a local R - action on the tangent bundle

annotations.note: The geodesic flow on the tangent bundle is not formalized.

annotations.label: Closed orbit = closed geodesic

anchor.snippet: A closed orbit of the geodesic flow corresponds to a closed geodesic on M

annotations.note: Closed geodesics and the geodesic flow are not formalized.

annotations.label: Geodesic flow as Hamiltonian flow

anchor.snippet: the geodesic flow is identified with a Hamiltonian flow on the cotangent bundle

annotations.note: The Hamiltonian description of geodesic flow is not formalized.

annotations.label: Geodesic flow preserves the metric

anchor.snippet: In particular the flow preserves the (pseudo-)Riemannian metric

annotations.note: Geodesic flow and its metric-preservation are not formalized.

annotations.label: Liouville's theorem: invariance of kinematic measure

anchor.snippet: Liouville's theorem implies invariance of a kinematic measure on the unit tangent bundle

annotations.note: The dynamical Liouville theorem on the unit tangent bundle is not formalized.

annotations.label: Geodesic spray

anchor.snippet: known as the geodesic spray

annotations.note: The geodesic spray is not formalized.

annotations.label: Splitting of double tangent bundle

anchor.snippet: an affine connection gives rise to a splitting of the double tangent bundle

annotations.note: The horizontal/vertical splitting of the double tangent bundle is not formalized.

annotations.label: Geodesic spray as unique horizontal vector field

anchor.snippet: The geodesic spray is the unique horizontal vector field W satisfying

annotations.note: The geodesic spray and its uniqueness are not formalized.

annotations.label: Spray from rescaling-equivariant connection

anchor.snippet: it is enough that the connection be equivariant under positive rescalings

annotations.note: Sprays from rescaling-equivariant connections are not formalized.

annotations.label: Invariance under affine reparameterization

anchor.snippet: Equation ( 1 ) is invariant under affine reparameterizations

annotations.note: The geodesic equation and its affine-reparameterization invariance are not formalized.

annotations.label: Geodesics with affine parameter

anchor.snippet: solutions of ( 1 ) are called geodesics with affine parameter

annotations.note: Affinely parameterized geodesics are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Connection determined by geodesics up to torsion

anchor.snippet: An affine connection is determined by its family of affinely parameterized geodesics, up to torsion

annotations.note: The torsion of a connection is formalized, but the statement relating it to determination by geodesics is not (geodesics are undefined).

annotations.label: Skew-symmetric difference tensor gives same geodesics

anchor.snippet: have the same geodesics, with the same affine parameterizations

annotations.note: The difference tensor of two connections is formalized, but the claim that a skew-symmetric difference yields the same geodesics is not.

annotations.label: Unique torsion-free connection with same geodesics

anchor.snippet: there is a unique connection having the same geodesics as

annotations.note: Torsion of connections is formalized, but the existence of a unique torsion-free connection with the same geodesics is not.

annotations.label: Projective connection

anchor.snippet: Geodesics without a particular parameterization are described by a projective connection

annotations.note: Projective connections are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Ribbon test

anchor.snippet: A ribbon "test" is a way of finding a geodesic on a physical surface

annotations.note: The ribbon test is not formalized.

annotations.label: Ribbon around a cone

anchor.snippet: when a ribbon is wound as a ring around a cone

annotations.note: This ribbon-on-cone example is not formalized.

annotations.label: Mathematical formulation of ribbon test

anchor.snippet: Mathematically the ribbon test can be formulated as finding a mapping

annotations.note: The mapping formulation of the ribbon test is not formalized.

annotations.label: Unique closed geodesic per homotopy class

anchor.snippet: any (free) homotopy class determines a unique (closed) geodesic for a hyperbolic metric

annotations.note: Closed geodesics and their uniqueness per homotopy class are not formalized.
